---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Libraries
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(skimr)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(naniar)   # for missing value visualizations
library(here)
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Cmd+Option+I*.
```{r}

#Preprocessing
#Load File

df <- read_excel("/Users/jamesshoenhair/Documents/ADS503FinalProject_Mice_Protein_Expression/Data_Cortex_Nuclear.xls")


dim(df)          # 1080 rows, 82 columns
head(df)
str(df)

#initial Summary Statistics

summary(df)
skim(df) 

#Check for Null Values
colSums(is.na(df))

df %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "column", values_to = "missing") %>%
  filter(missing > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(missing))

#Handling Null Values Through Imputation

df_imputed <- df %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ifelse(is.na(.), median(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

```


```{r}
#Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
#Updated Summary Statistics
skim(df)

#Dat set Class Distributions
df %>%
  count(class) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = class, y = n, fill = class)) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Sample Count by Class")

#Genotype/Treatment/Behavior

table(df$Genotype)
table(df$Treatment)
table(df$Behavior)

#Protein Expression Distributions

df %>%
  select(DYRK1A_N, BDNF_N, APP_N, Tau_N) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything()) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = name)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Protein Expression Distributions")

#Correlation Heatmap
  
numeric_cols <- df %>% select(where(is.numeric))
corrplot(cor(numeric_cols, use = "complete.obs"),
         method = "color", tl.cex = 0.4, order = "hclust")

```

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Cmd+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file). 

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
